type: SURVEY
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding opt-in disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header (b'DSPC') framing for Cache.put/get, plus cull_limit/eviction_policy knobs. Touches Cache.__init__ (new params: compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy), Cache.put (compress+prepend header before disk write), Cache.get (detect header, decompress), possibly new set_compression() method. Also touches configure_cache() in __init__.py to add compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params and pass through to Cache(...).

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding TTL support: Cache.__init__ (new ttl param), Cache.get (lazy purge disk_cache.expire()), Cache.put (disk_cache.set with expire=ttl), configure_cache() (new ttl param), DSPY_CACHE instantiation (add ttl=None), new set_ttl() helper function, __all__ list in dspy/clients/__init__.py

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
region: Cache.__init__
code:     def __init__(
        self,
        enable_disk_cache: bool,
        enable_memory_cache: bool,
        disk_cache_dir: str,
        disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = 1024 * 1024 * 10,
        memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
        ttl: Optional[int] = None,
        compress: bool = False,
        cull_limit: int = 10,
        eviction_policy: str = "least-recently-stored",
    ):
        """
        Args:
            enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
            enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
            disk_cache_dir: The directory where the disk cache is stored.
            disk_size_limit_bytes: The maximum size of the disk cache (in bytes).
            memory_max_entries: The maximum size of the in-memory cache (in number of items).
            ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
            compress: Whether to compress values before writing them to the on-disk cache.
            cull_limit: The number of items culled from the on-disk cache when it exceeds its size limit.
            eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the on-disk cache.
        """

        self.enable_disk_cache = enable_disk_cache
        self.enable_memory_cache = enable_memory_cache
        self.ttl = ttl
        self.compress = compress
        if self.enable_memory_cache:
            if ttl is not None:
                self.memory_cache = TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl)
            else:
                self.memory_cache = LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries)
        else:
            self.memory_cache = {}
        if self.enable_disk_cache:
            self.disk_cache = FanoutCache(
                shards=16,
                timeout=10,
                directory=disk_cache_dir,
                size_limit=disk_size_limit_bytes,
                cull_limit=cull_limit,
                eviction_policy=eviction_policy,
            )
        else:
            self.disk_cache = {}

        self._lock = threading.RLock()
note: proposal, please counter if your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy naming differs

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
region: Cache.__init__
code:     def __init__(
        self,
        enable_disk_cache: bool,
        enable_memory_cache: bool,
        disk_cache_dir: str,
        disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = 1024 * 1024 * 10,
        memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
        compress: Optional[str] = None,
        cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
        eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
        ttl: Optional[int] = None,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
            enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
            disk_cache_dir: The directory where the disk cache is stored.
            disk_size_limit_bytes: The maximum size of the disk cache (in bytes).
            memory_max_entries: The maximum size of the in-memory cache (in number of items).
            compress: The compression codec used for on-disk cache entries. One of "gzip", "zlib", or
                None to disable compression.
            cull_limit: The number of entries culled from the disk cache each time it exceeds its size limit.
                If None, the diskcache default is used.
            eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the disk cache (e.g. "least-recently-stored").
                If None, the diskcache default is used.
            ttl: The time-to-live, in seconds, for on-disk cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
        """

        self.enable_disk_cache = enable_disk_cache
        self.enable_memory_cache = enable_memory_cache
        self.compress = compress
        self.ttl = ttl
        if self.enable_memory_cache:
            self.memory_cache = LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries)
        else:
            self.memory_cache = {}
        if self.enable_disk_cache:
            disk_cache_settings = {}
            if cull_limit is not None:
                disk_cache_settings["cull_limit"] = cull_limit
            if eviction_policy is not None:
                disk_cache_settings["eviction_policy"] = eviction_policy
            self.disk_cache = FanoutCache(
                shards=16,
                timeout=10,
                directory=disk_cache_dir,
                size_limit=disk_size_limit_bytes,
                **disk_cache_settings,
            )
        else:
            self.disk_cache = {}

        self._lock = threading.RLock()
note: Merged __init__ with both compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy (mine) and ttl (yours). Let me know if you need ttl handled differently here.

note: Same as your draft, but memory_cache now switches to TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) when ttl is set (my feature requires the memory layer to actually honor ttl, not just self.ttl bookkeeping for disk expire()). Everything else identical to your draft.

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
region: Cache.put
code:     def put(
        self,
        request: Dict[str, Any],
        value: Any,
        ignored_args_for_cache_key: Optional[list[str]] = None,
        enable_memory_cache: bool = True,
    ) -> None:
        try:
            key = self.cache_key(request, ignored_args_for_cache_key)
        except Exception:
            logger.debug(f"Failed to generate cache key for request: {request}")
            return

        if self.enable_memory_cache and enable_memory_cache:
            with self._lock:
                self.memory_cache[key] = value

        if self.enable_disk_cache:
            try:
                self.disk_cache.set(key, self._encode_value(value), expire=self.ttl)
            except Exception as e:
                # Disk cache writing can fail for different reasons, e.g. disk full or the  is not picklable.
                logger.debug(f"Failed to put value in disk cache: {value}, {e}")
note: Switched from __setitem__ to .set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) since __setitem__ doesn't accept expire. self.ttl defaults to None (no expiry) so this is a no-op behavior change when ttl unused. _encode_value() (mine) compresses+headers the pickled value before write.

note: Resending cleanly (previous send may have had a shell quoting glitch). Switched from dict-style assignment to disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) since setitem does not accept expire. self.ttl None means no expiry, so this is a no-op when ttl is unused. encode_value() (mine) compresses and headers the pickled value before write.

files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
region: configure_cache()
code: def configure_cache(
    enable_disk_cache: Optional[bool] = True,
    enable_memory_cache: Optional[bool] = True,
    disk_cache_dir: Optional[str] = DISK_CACHE_DIR,
    disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = DISK_CACHE_LIMIT,
    memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
    enable_litellm_cache: bool = False,
    compress: Optional[str] = None,
    cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
    eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
    ttl: Optional[int] = None,
):
    """Configure the cache for DSPy.

    Args:
        enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
        enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
        disk_cache_dir: The directory to store the on-disk cache.
        disk_size_limit_bytes: The size limit of the on-disk cache.
        memory_max_entries: The maximum number of entries in the in-memory cache.
        enable_litellm_cache: Whether to enable LiteLLM cache.
        compress: The compression codec used for on-disk cache entries. One of "gzip", "zlib", or
            None to disable compression.
        cull_limit: The number of entries culled from the disk cache each time it exceeds its size limit.
            If None, the diskcache default is used.
        eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the disk cache (e.g. "least-recently-stored").
            If None, the diskcache default is used.
        ttl: The time-to-live, in seconds, for on-disk cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
    """
    if enable_disk_cache and enable_litellm_cache:
        raise ValueError(
            "Cannot enable both LiteLLM and DSPy on-disk cache, please set at most one of `enable_disk_cache` or "
            "`enable_litellm_cache` to True."
        )

    if enable_litellm_cache:
        try:
            litellm.cache = LitellmCache(disk_cache_dir=DISK_CACHE_DIR, type="disk")

            if litellm.cache.cache.disk_cache.size_limit != DISK_CACHE_LIMIT:
                litellm.cache.cache.disk_cache.reset("size_limit", DISK_CACHE_LIMIT)
        except Exception as e:
            # It's possible that users don't have the write permissions to the cache directory.
            # In that case, we'll just disable the cache.
            logger.warning("Failed to initialize LiteLLM cache: %s", e)
            litellm.cache = None
    else:
        litellm.cache = None

    import dspy

    dspy.cache = Cache(
        enable_disk_cache,
        enable_memory_cache,
        disk_cache_dir,
        disk_size_limit_bytes,
        memory_max_entries,
        compress=compress,
        cull_limit=cull_limit,
        eviction_policy=eviction_policy,
        ttl=ttl,
    )
note: Added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy (mine) plus ttl (yours) as new keyword params, forwarded to Cache(...) via keyword args so ordering stays stable regardless of how many new params either of us adds later.

    """Configure the cache for DSPy.

    Args:

        cull_limit: The number of entries culled from the disk cache each time it exceeds its size limit.
            If None, the diskcache default is used.
        eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the disk cache (e.g. "least-recently-stored").
            If None, the diskcache default is used.
        ttl: The time-to-live, in seconds, for on-disk cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
    """
    if enable_disk_cache and enable_litellm_cache:
        raise ValueError(
            "Cannot enable both LiteLLM and DSPy on-disk cache, please set at most one of `enable_disk_cache` or "

        )

    if enable_litellm_cache:

        enable_disk_cache,
        enable_memory_cache,
        disk_cache_dir,
        disk_size_limit_bytes,
        memory_max_entries,
        compress=compress,
        cull_limit=cull_limit,
        eviction_policy=eviction_policy,
        ttl=ttl,
    )

note: Since our agreed get()/put() call self._decode_value / self._encode_value, I need the exact verbatim code of those two helper methods (and any compression-related class attrs/constants like the magic header) to include in my own patch too, so my patch.txt is self-contained and doesn't AttributeError when compress is unused. files: dspy/clients/cache.py
region: _encode_value/_decode_value/set_compression helper methods
code:     _MAGIC_HEADER = b"DSPC"
    _CODEC_NONE = 0x00
    _CODEC_GZIP = 0x01
    _CODEC_ZLIB = 0x02
    _CODEC_BY_NAME = {"gzip": _CODEC_GZIP, "zlib": _CODEC_ZLIB}

    def set_compression(self, compress: Optional[str]) -> None:
        """Dynamically change the compression codec used for on-disk cache entries.

        Args:
            compress: The compression codec to use. One of "gzip", "zlib", or None to disable compression.
        """
        if compress is not None and compress not in self._CODEC_BY_NAME:
            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported compression codec: {compress!r}. Must be one of 'gzip', 'zlib', or None.")
        self.compress = compress

    def _encode_value(self, value: Any) -> bytes:
        """Pickle, optionally compress, and prepend a magic header to a value before writing it to disk."""
        payload = cloudpickle.dumps(value)
        codec = self._CODEC_BY_NAME.get(self.compress, self._CODEC_NONE)
        if codec == self._CODEC_GZIP:
            payload = gzip.compress(payload)
        elif codec == self._CODEC_ZLIB:
            payload = zlib.compress(payload)
        return self._MAGIC_HEADER + bytes([codec]) + payload

    def _decode_value(self, raw: Any) -> Any:
        """Decode a value read from disk, transparently handling legacy header-less entries."""
        if not isinstance(raw, (bytes, bytearray)) or raw[:4] != self._MAGIC_HEADER:
            # Pre-existing cache entry written before compression support was added.
            return raw

        codec = raw[4]
        payload = raw[5:]
        if codec == self._CODEC_GZIP:
            payload = gzip.decompress(payload)
        elif codec == self._CODEC_ZLIB:
            payload = zlib.decompress(payload)
        return cloudpickle.loads(payload)
